en: Manual handling of loads must be minimized wherever feasible. Organizational measures and mechanical aids must be prioritized to reduce manual lifting or carrying.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 64(2)
Manual handling hazard assessments must address load characteristics, physical effort, environment, and task demands.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 64(3)
Controls for manual handling risks must reduce musculoskeletal hazards considering environment and task demands.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 64(3)
Control Measures (Elimination, Engineering, Administrative, and/or Personal Protective Equipment)
Storage arrangements must be made to prevent employee danger from stored goods, containers, or packaging.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 10(1)
Permissible floor loads, storage equipment loads, and container filling limits must not be exceeded.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 10(2)

Load handlers must be physically fit and trained before performing manual handling tasks. Manual handling risk information must address musculoskeletal hazards and the consequences of improper handling.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 64(4-5)
Proper load handling instruction must address safe techniques and hazard awareness.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 64(5)
